title: eleusine indica subsp. africana
intro: Eleusine indica subsp. africana, also known as finger grass or wild millet, is a species of grass native to Africa, belonging to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Eleusine indica subsp. africana is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
